 physically can=E2=80=99t be as smooth, accurate, or instant as Ramp.

 company slash corporate card (and not the other way around [https://email.=

t (or don=E2=80=99t), like:=C2=A0

mit via text, or anything else that a company might want to trigger =F0=9F=
=98=AF
Automatically match receipts in the moment or months later =F0=9F=98=AE

n page=C2=A0 =F0=9F=98=B2

 at the point-of-sale, before it ever goes through =F0=9F=A4=AF

tically saved consumers money by scan=
ning their emails for potential savings on the things they bought. Capital =
One acquired the company in 2016, and by the time Eric and Karim left, they=
 were saving consumers over $100 million per year. But the mission wasn&#82=
17;t complete. They wanted to see if they could help <em>companies</em> sav=
e money, too. </p><p style=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-size: 16px; line-he=
ight: 26px; margin: 0 0 1em 0;">To make the mission a reality, Eric and Kar=

em 0;"><strong>Team. </strong>Ramp’s team backs up VC Guide’s s=

r classes at MIT, Harvard and Stanford, were top ranked in the world at mat=
hematics, and previously worked at places like Jane Street, Facebook AI Res=
earch, Google Research, and more. The Design team hails from the School of =
Visual Arts, Facebook and Ideo. </p><p style=3D"color: #1a1a1a; font-s=
ize: 16px; line-height: 26px; margin: 0 0 1em 0;">Eric and Karim attracted =
top talent like Nik Koblov (former VP of Engineering at Affirm), Srinath Sr=
inivasan (former Head of Credit Strategy at Goldman’s Marcus and Appl=
e Card), and Miriam Mark (former VP of Sales at WeWork). As Ramp grows at b=
reakneck speed, it’s hiring people from leading companies like Uber, =
Plaid, Namely, Fundera, Square, and Not Boring favorite, Slack. </p><p=
